docs.title: EzChat Documentation
docs.text: EzChat is a communication management plugin for Minecraft servers, designed to give administrators control over player messaging while keeping chat systems easy to use.

docs.title: Main Features
docs.text:
• Global and private communication controls
  • In-game mail support for offline messaging
  • Logging and audit tools for moderation
  • Mute and temporary mute system
  • Permission-based access for staff tools

Use this documentation to set up EzChat and learn how to configure commands, permissions, logs, and moderation settings.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: EzChat provides commands for chat control, private messaging, mail, logs, and moderation.

docs.title: Player Commands
docs.text:
• /togglechat — Toggle global chat visibility for yourself.
  • /togglemsg — Enable or disable private messages.
  • /togglemail — Enable or disable mail notifications/messages.
  • /msg <player> <message> — Send a private message.
  • /reply <message> — Reply to the last private message.
  • /mail <player> <message> — Send mail to a player.

docs.title: Staff / Admin Commands
docs.text:
• /ezchat logs — View communication and audit logs.
  • /ezchat mute <player> <reason> — Permanently mute a player.
  • /ezchat mutetemp <player> <duration> <reason> — Temporarily mute a player.

Note: Exact argument formats may vary by plugin version and command aliases.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: EzChat uses multiple configuration files to separate features clearly.

docs.title: Configuration Files
docs.text:
• config.yml — Main plugin settings and global behavior.
  • private-message.yml — Private message formatting and behavior.
  • mail.yml — Mail feature settings.
  • logs.yml — Logging configuration and retention behavior.
  • mute.yml — Mute system settings and defaults.

docs.title: Recommended Workflow
docs.text:
1. Stop the server before major configuration changes.
  2. Edit YAML files carefully (spaces only, no tabs).
  3. Save changes and restart the server.
  4. Verify behavior in-game and check console logs for errors.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Follow these steps to install EzChat on your server:

  1. Download the latest EzChat plugin JAR file.
  2. Stop your Minecraft server (recommended).
  3. Place the JAR file in your server's plugins/ folder.
  4. Start or restart the server.
  5. Confirm EzChat loads successfully in the console logs.

After startup, EzChat will generate its default configuration files in the plugin directory.

docs.title: Logging
docs.text: EzChat includes a logging system to help with moderation and communication review.

docs.title: Log Types
docs.text:
• Communication logs: Track player chat, private messages, and mail events.
  • Audit logs: Track moderation or administrative actions.

docs.title: Log Commands
docs.text:
• /ezchat logs — Open or view logs using in-game command output.

Depending on your setup, this command may support filters and page navigation.

docs.title: Pagination
docs.text: Large log outputs are typically split into pages for readability. Use command arguments (if available in your build) to move between pages or filter results.

docs.title: Mute System
docs.text: The mute system allows staff to limit player communication when rules are violated.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• /ezchat mute <player> <reason> — Apply a permanent mute.
  • /ezchat mutetemp <player> <duration> <reason> — Apply a temporary mute.

docs.title: Mute Effects
docs.text:
When a player is muted, communication actions may be blocked, including:

  • Public chat messages
  • Private messages
  • Mail usage (depending on configuration)

Muted players should receive feedback when they attempt restricted actions.

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text: Use permissions to control access to EzChat moderation and logging tools.

docs.title: Core Staff Permissions
docs.text:
• ezchat.logs — Access EzChat log viewing commands.
  • ezchat.mute — Use permanent mute command.
  • ezchat.mutetemp — Use temporary mute command.

Assign these permissions through your permissions plugin (for example, LuckPerms) to the appropriate staff roles.
